name: Octopus Deploy REST API
what_it_does: Enterprise deployment automation platform REST API for managing releases, deployments, environments, runbooks, and tenants — enabling agents to trigger deployments, query deployment status, and manage release pipelines across multiple environments.
use_cases: Triggering application deployments to specific environments programmatically via API
Querying deployment status and history for release management agent workflows
Managing tenant-specific deployments for multi-customer SaaS release automation
Executing operational runbooks for infrastructure maintenance via agent triggers
Integrating Octopus deployment status into CI/CD pipelines and chatbots
not_for: Simple single-environment deployments (overkill — use GitHub Actions)
Kubernetes-native GitOps workflows (use ArgoCD or Flux instead)
Container-only deployments without traditional app deployment needs
best_when: An agent manages complex multi-environment, multi-tenant deployment pipelines with approval gates and operational runbooks.
avoid_when: You need GitOps-style continuous delivery — Octopus Deploy is more traditional push-based deployment automation.

auth.notes: API key generated per user in Octopus UI. Passed as X-Octopus-ApiKey header. Service accounts supported with their own API keys. No OAuth2 — API key model. Space-scoped API access (multi-space instances require space specification).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10 targets (self-hosted); Octopus Cloud free trial
pricing.notes: Target-based pricing (deploy targets, not API calls). Self-hosted and cloud options. Popular in .NET enterprise and Windows deployment scenarios. Significant community and integrations ecosystem.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; self-hosted performance depends on server

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI/Swagger spec published. Comprehensive REST API documentation. Error responses are JSON with problem details. Async deployment — agents must poll task completion. Webhook notifications for deployment events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Release creation with same version is idempotent (returns existing). Deployment creation creates new deployment each call — use idempotency key pattern.
known_agent_gotchas: Multi-space instances require space ID in API calls — default space may not be correct
Deployment is async — agents must poll task log and status for completion
Manual intervention steps pause deployments — agents need human-approval handling
Tenant deployments require tenant-space relationship to be pre-configured

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. API key auth with header (more secure than query param). TLS enforced. Space-based multi-tenancy for isolation. Service accounts for automation. Self-hosted option for highest security control.
